name: The Four Lokapalas (Directional Guardians)
shortDescription: The Four Lokapalas are powerful celestial kings who guard the four cardinal directions in Buddhist cosmology, protecting the world from evil.
description: The Four Lokapalas, also known as the Four Heavenly Kings or Caturmaharajika, are important figures in Buddhist cosmology. They are powerful deities who reside on the lower slopes of Mount Meru and are tasked with guarding the four cardinal directions, ensuring the protection of the Dharma and the world from evil influences. Each Lokapala is associated with a specific direction, color, weapon, and retinue of celestial beings. Dhrtarastra guards the East, often depicted playing a pipa or lute, symbolizing the harmony and balance he maintains. Virudhaka guards the South, holding a sword, representing the protection of the Dharma and the punishment of evil. Virupaksha guards the West, often shown holding a stupa or a snake, symbolizing faith and transformation. Vaishravana guards the North, holding a pagoda or a victory banner, symbolizing wealth and victory over evil. They are typically depicted in armor and are revered as protectors of temples and monasteries, ensuring the safety and prosperity of the Buddhist community. Their images are frequently found at the entrances of Buddhist temples, serving as a reminder of the constant vigilance against negativity and the importance of upholding moral principles. They also serve as protectors of Mount Meru.
appearance: Depicted as armored celestial kings, each with distinct weapons and attributes.
